Inntektsrapportering for aktivitetspenger (#1271)

### **Behov / Bakgrunn**

Legger til støtte for å rapportere inntekt for aktivitetspenger,
inkludert asynkron persistering til Abakus.

### **Løsning**

- `DokumentMottakerInntektrapportering` og
`AktivitetspengerInntektrapporteringValidator` for
aktivitetspenger-spesifikk dokumentmottak og validering
- `InntektsrapporteringAsyncPersisterer` i felles mottak-modul håndterer
to utfall i `opprettTaskForPersistering`:
- **Tom inntekt** (ingen arbeidstaker/frilans-perioder): setter
dokumentstatus direkte til `GYLDIG`, oppretter ingen prosess-task
- **Har inntekt**: setter dokumentstatus til `BEHANDLER` og oppretter
`AsyncAbakusLagreOpptjeningTask` med journalpost-id og brevkode som
properties
- Brevkode-støtte og journalpostvurdering utvidet for ny dokumenttype

### **Andre endringer**

- Enhetstester (`InntektsrapporteringAsyncPersistererTest`) med Mockito
dekker begge utfall i `opprettTaskForPersistering`
- k9format-versjon oppdatert

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix bugs and improve robustness across backend and extension

Backend:
- Prevent UCI command injection via newline sanitization in setOption()
- Validate depth range (0 or 1-50) to prevent extreme engine searches
- Fix broadcast race condition by snapshotting wss.clients with Array.from()
- Add input validation to ECO lookup (guard against null/undefined)
- Fix findFirstBook returning empty string instead of null when no book found
- Close existing book handles before re-init to prevent resource leaks
- Add try-catch around Lichess API JSON parsing for malformed responses
- Validate search limits to reject negative/NaN movetime and node values

Extension:
- Add validation to uciToSquares() with null guards at all call sites
- Fix en passant detection bug in applyUciMove() (was checking post-move state)
- Reset dragHandlersAttached on SPA navigation so listeners bind to new board
- Reset voice announcement state (lastSpokenMove/lastSpokenOpening) on new game
- Use renderGeneration counter to discard stale bestmove overlays

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix WeakMap/WeakSet constructors and cross-realm BigInt JSON panic

- WeakMap/WeakSet: route NewCall through call_builtin (like Map/Set) so
  constructor creates VmObject wrapper with __map_data__/__set_data__,
  __type__, and correct prototype chain. Previous code pushed bare VmMap/
  VmSet values without prototype, causing method calls to fail.

- Add BUILTIN_CTOR_WEAKMAP and BUILTIN_CTOR_WEAKSET handlers in
  call_builtin with full iterable consumption support.

- Fix cross-realm BigInt JSON.stringify panic: guard register_cross_realm_fn
  against remapping parent-realm functions stored on child-realm objects
  (ip >= child.chunk.code.len() check).

- Fix value display: add WeakMap/WeakSet/Map/Set type detection in both
  value_to_string and value_to_compact_result_string.

- Restore simple_object_method_source helper in compiler.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Clamp jacket tone to closest allowed tier for multi-tone

When --tone pop,mainstream is specified, jacket generation picks
the closest allowed tier to the subtitle's actual score instead of
falling back to auto-detection. E.g. a niche-scoring subtitle with
--tone pop,mainstream gets clamped to mainstream.

commit.message:
Add x:Code directive support for inline C# in XAML (#34715)

## Description

Implements the `x:Code` XAML directive, allowing inline C# method
definitions directly in XAML files. This complements the existing XEXPR
(C# Expressions) feature.

Fixes #34712

## What it does

`x:Code` lets you define methods inline in XAML:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage">
    <x:Code><![CDATA[
        void OnButtonClicked(object sender, EventArgs e)
        {
            // inline C# method
        }
    ]]></x:Code>
    <Button Clicked="OnButtonClicked" Text="Click me" />
</ContentPage>
```

## Constraints

- **Requires XSG (XAML Source Generator)** — not supported by Runtime
inflation or XamlC
- **Requires `EnablePreviewFeatures`** — same gate as XEXPR
- **Must be a direct child of the root element** (WPF parity)
- **Must use CDATA** for the code block content
- **Must have `x:Class`** on the root element

## Architecture

x:Code is implemented as a **third source generator pipeline** alongside
CodeBehind (CB) and InitializeComponent (IC):

```
CB → x:Code → IC
```

- `ComputeXCodeSource()` extracts code blocks from parsed XAML
- `XCodeCodeWriter` emits a bare partial class (namespace + class +
verbatim code, no usings)
- The emitted syntax trees feed into the compilation before IC runs
- All IC visitors skip x:Code elements via
`GeneratorHelpers.IsXCodeElement()`

Output hint name format: `{path}_{FileName}.xaml.xcode.cs`

## Diagnostics

| ID | Condition |
|----|-----------|
| MAUIX2012 | `EnablePreviewFeatures` not set (reused from XEXPR) |
| MAUIX2015 | x:Code not a direct child of root element |
| MAUIX2016 | x:Code used without `x:Class` |

## Tests

- **6 SourceGen unit tests** — happy path, multiple blocks, no CDATA,
diagnostics, no-op
- **4 Xaml.UnitTests** — SourceGen success, Runtime failure, XamlC
failure, MockSourceGenerator no-diagnostics

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(watcher): add persistent/ephemeral modes with restart support

Persistent watchers (--persistent) survive tmux/system restarts:
- Auto-resurrected on startup via 'pilot resurrect-watchers'
- Notify on state transitions only (OK↔FAIL) to avoid spam
- Stay in DB after stopping, can be restarted with R in TUI

Ephemeral watchers (default) auto-delete from DB on completion.

TUI improvements:
- 🔄/⚡ icons distinguish persistent vs ephemeral
- R key to restart stopped watchers
- d key to delete watcher entries
- Auto-refresh every 5 seconds
- Name shown prominently in list

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix 3 PathMaker bugs: Manufacturing workspace design detection, Forge Vault token fallback, stock W×H×D dimensions

- handlers.py: Replace app.activeProduct with activeDocument.products
  .itemByProductType('DesignProductType') so 'No active design' crash
  no longer fires when user is in the Manufacturing workspace (CAM
  product was being returned instead of the Design product)

- handlers.py: Add GenerateCamInputChangedHandler to toggle stock
  dimension inputs; Add Stock Mode dropdown (Relative Offset / Fixed
  Dimensions) with three W×H×D mm fields that appear when Fixed is
  selected — no second body needed

- cam_generator.py: Complete _create_setup() fixed_size branch —
  apply W×H×D via Fusion CAM parameters API (job_stocksizex/y/z)
  after setup creation

- setup_wizard.py: Add _detect_ai_token() that queries Forge Vault
  HTTP API at localhost:3005 as a fallback when wizard is launched
  outside Forge Terminal; step 5 auto-detects token in both cases

- setup_wizard.py: Update step 7 to clarify stock mode options and
  explicitly state no second body is needed

- Bump wizard version to 1.0.8

commit.message:
Show existing files (#8697)

## Trello card URL


https://trello.com/c/LuBIi32E/2733-move-attachment-antivirus-scanning-checks-from-google-drive-to-azure

## Changes in this PR:
This PR shows the uploaded files for the uploaded job applications for
both the jobseeker and hiring staff journeys.

It also adds functionality to delete attachments in the:
- religious job application flow to allow jobseekers to delete baptism
certificates
- the uploaded application flow to allow jobseekers to delete uploaded
application forms
- the vacancy creation flow with uploaded application form to allow
hiring staff to delete uploaded their own application forms

This is being done as the first step towards implementing the azure
defender antivirus to replace our existing google drive strategy as,
going forward, we will want users to delete their own files where
possible if they are flagged as malicious and prior to this change users
did not have the ability to delete these uploaded files.

## Screenshots of UI changes:

### Before

### After


## Checklists:

### Data & Schema Changes

If this PR modifies data structures or validations, check the following:

- [ ] Adds/removes model validations
- [ ] Adds/removes database fields
- [ ] Modifies Vacancy enumerables (phases, working patterns, job roles,
key stages, etc.)

<details>
<summary>If any of the above options has changed then the author must
check/resolve all of the following...</summary>

### Integration Impact

Does this change affect any of these integrations?
- [ ] DfE Analytics platform
- [ ] Legacy imports mappings
- [ ] DWP Find a Job export mappings
- [ ] Publisher ATS API (may require mapping updates or API versioning)

### User Experience & Data Integrity

Could this change impact:
- [ ] Existing subscription alerts (will legacy subscription search
filters break?)
- [ ] Legacy vacancy copying (will copied vacancies fail new
validations?)
- [ ] In-progress drafts for Vacancies or Job Applications
</details>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Marc Sardón <marc.sardon@education.gov.uk>

commit.message:
feat: pause/resume sync at the protocol level

- SyncClient.pause(): closes WebSocket, stops auto-sync timer, sets
  paused flag that blocks connect/syncNow/handleMessage
- SyncClient.resume(): clears flag, reconnects, flushes pending edits
- React wrapper exposes pauseSync()/resumeSync() with 'paused' status
- App.tsx toggle uses pause/resume instead of disconnect/connect
- E2E test: paused client does not receive edits, resume catches up

Add scripts/ folder with Bash (.sh) and PowerShell (.ps1) pairs:

- start-server: parameterized launcher (plain/tls/both, HLS, auth)
- test-e2e: 7 E2E tests (RTMP basic, RTMPS, HLS hook, auth allow/reject, RTMPS+auth)

Fix ShellHook to detect Windows and use powershell.exe instead of /bin/bash.

commit.message:
fix(pihole): remove drop:ALL, keep default root capabilities

Pi-hole FTL needs DAC_OVERRIDE, FOWNER, CHOWN, SETGID, SETUID and
more to manage its log files and pid files. The network namespace
enforces privileged PSA so keeping default root capabilities is
safe and avoids capability whack-a-mole.

Add Ch.8 Loss Function Optimality post (163)

- 신규: 163-loss-function-optimality-tests.qmd
  - Ch.8.3.5 가설검정의 손실함수 최적성
  - 0-1 손실, 일반화 0-1 손실 정의
  - 위험함수 = c_I*beta(theta) / c_{II}*(1-beta(theta)) 유도
  - UMP 검정 위험함수 예시 및 theta_0 불연속 해석
  - 비대칭 연속 손실 (식 8.3.13) 실무 예시
  - Ch.7.3.4 점추정 손실함수와 비교표
  - 비율 c_II/c_I 에 따른 최적 유의수준 코드

commit.message:
Update the skill files (#923)

* Update the skills

* Update the skill files

* Update the skills and add verification-campaigns.instructions.md

* Update the skill files

* Update the skill files

* Mention congr in the maxRecDepth issues

* Update the skills

* skills: ban `cases` with named constructor arms — use cdot blocks

Named constructor arms (`| Foo => ...`) in `cases ... with` break
incrementality by forcing all branches into a single elaboration unit.
Use cdot (`·`) blocks instead, which are independently elaborated.

Added to both aeneas-lean-core (banned tactics table + dedicated section)
and aeneas-tactics-quickref (banned patterns section + table).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
